response:
Sec. 1  
Allergies to peanuts are immune system responses to proteins found in peanuts  
These responses can lead to severe reactions such as anaphylaxis  
Living underwater does not eliminate exposure to peanut proteins  
Peanut proteins can be present in food or environmental sources even in aquatic settings  
Allergic reactions are triggered by the body not by the environment  

Sec. 2  
Underwater living does not prevent the body from reacting to allergens  
Medical treatment for allergies is not dependent on location  
Allergic individuals must avoid allergens regardless of their surroundings  
There is no known method to make underwater environments safe for peanut allergic individuals  
The question is based on a misunderstanding of how allergies function
